verdict.answer_first: A and B both lead with the answer: A cites "No — this check has a serious security flaw"; B cites "No — it has a serious flaw". A is slightly clearer because it names "this check" immediately.
verdict.decisiveness: B is more disciplined: "The fix that matters most is the first one" keeps one priority; A adds "argon2 (preferred), scrypt, or bcrypt" plus a follow-up offer.
verdict.fabrication: B overstates slightly with "only works if you're storing raw passwords"; A qualifies it as "plaintext (or otherwise in a directly-comparable form)". No hard fabricated numbers from either.
verdict.actionability: B is easier to act on because it gives the replacement line directly: "const ok = await bcrypt.compare(...)" and keeps the rest secondary.
